name: Semantic Arch Card Search
description: Embedding-based (vector) search over architecture cards, surfaced in the ArchPanel search box. The detector mcp-core/arch-search.mjs reads cards via the shared arch-cards.mjs reader (same loader arch-server.mjs uses), flattens each card (name + description + files + guidelines + anti_patterns + decisions + dependsOn + tags) into one embedding document, and ranks them against the query via semantic-search.mjs semanticScores() (local all-MiniLM-L6-v2 through @xenova/transformers, mean-pooled + L2-normalized cosine, on-disk vector cache). Cards below the MIN_SCORE relevance floor (0.25 cosine) are dropped, so an off-topic query legitimately returns [] and the panel's empty state is reachable. Two modes: one-shot CLI (--query/--root/--limit, prints a JSON array of {slug, score} best-first, [] when the model is unavailable) and --serve — a long-lived JSON-lines worker ({id, root, query, limit} in, {id, results}/{id, error} out) that keeps the model warm; it drains in-flight requests before exiting on stdin close. The extension talks to it via scan-runner.cjs createArchSearchWorker (lazy spawn, pending-map by id, re-spawn on crash, 120s/request timeout, dispose() kills the child) held as a singleton in scanHost.searchArchCards and disposed via ctx.subscriptions; the one-shot runArchSearch runner remains for non-interactive callers. In the UI, ArchPanel debounces the query (200ms), clears the previous ranking immediately on query change (substring order is the interim state), then orders cards by the returned ranking plus any substring matches, showing a "semantic" badge; an empty result falls back to substring filtering.
guidelines: arch-search.mjs must print [] (not error) when semanticScores returns null so callers substring-fall-back — same as code-search.
Use isCliEntry() from cli-entry.mjs for the CLI entrypoint guard — do not hand-copy the realpath/basename/pathToFileURL idiom.
Read cards through arch-cards.mjs readArchCards so BM25 arch_search and semantic search always see the same card set.
Interactive (per-keystroke) callers must go through createArchSearchWorker, not runArchSearch — a one-shot spawn reloads the embedding model every query.
New detector CLIs need: an esbuild mcp-server/* entry, an mcp-core package.json export (./arch-search.mjs), and a scan-runner runner + its .d.ts/.d.cts decl.
anti_patterns: Don't embed cards on the extension host — the model + transformers live in the spawned Node detector process (ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE).
Don't require @xenova/transformers — it's an optional external dep; absence must degrade to substring search, not crash.
Don't return the full ranked corpus without the MIN_SCORE floor — the UI relies on the ranking layer owning the relevance cutoff.
decisions: The relevance cutoff (MIN_SCORE = 0.25 cosine) lives in arch-search.mjs, not the UI — one owner of 'what counts as a match'; the earlier keep-half UI heuristic was removed.
Per-keystroke search uses a persistent --serve worker (JSON lines over stdio) so the MiniLM model loads once per extension-host lifetime instead of per query.
